channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Wooden Slats dumped in Kent Road, 5th April
item.description:
Next to house number 22 Kent Road thx
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 24, Kent Road, Poverest, Orpington, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Extremely dangerous parking, 5th April
item.description:
Dangerous parking by flat bed trucks, I am requesting a TRO to extend the double yellow lines in the name of safety and common sense. These ignorant ch*vs are clearly running a business from a residential building and flouting the councils parking rules (5.18) The council contractor NSL will only act if it can make money hence the need for double yellow lines.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Guibal Road, Horn Park, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: No service, just dump., 5th April
item.description:
Your service is wrong as those bins in Christ Church way are here since ages. The area is dirty and smelling.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Christchurch Way, Greenwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dumped bed and mattress, 5th April
item.description:
Bed frame and mattress dumped on grass, large amount of litter
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Hockenden Lane, St Mary Cray, Swanley
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Overhang tree, 5th April
item.description:
Fallen tree overhanging in carriageway

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Goldfinch Road, Plumstead, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Bins left on the street 24 seven, 5th April
item.description:
These bins are left on the street 24 seven and are now blocking the pavement so anyone in a wheelchair or with a buggy can’t get past they have enough room to store their bins at the front of the building but they leave them on the street.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Bethune Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dead fox, 5th April
item.description:
Dead fox bagged up outside of property. Needs to be collected asap.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Beechfield Road, Widmore, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Lots of vandalism, 5th April
item.description:
Please cleam

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 332, Camberwell New Road, Camberwell, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Outside 59 Brondesbury Road, 5th April
item.description:
A man approached 59 Brondesbury Road on the evening of 4 April 2026 and dumped two blue and black rugs outside the front wall.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Brondesbury Road, London
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Fly-tip Small - Less than one bag

item.title: Rubbish at litter bin, 5th April
item.description:
Please put big communal bin there for residents. Food shops right nearby - unhygienic and unhealthy.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 352-356, Lordship Lane, East Dulwich, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Bin left out by 32 ladbrooke crescent, 5th April
item.description:
Bin been left out by 32 ladbrooke on the pavement for over a week
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Ladbrooke Crescent, Albany Park, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Flytip, 5th April
item.description:
2 large metallic microwaves (I think) on pavement.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Downs Road, Beckenham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Large Scale Damage, 5th April
item.description:
Large amount of damage on the pavement, multiple steel bollards knocked over, and concrete debris everywhere (as of around 04:00am 05/04/26). Presumably a vehicle has caused this but no damaged vehicles seen or any police activity. I would see if the CCTV just up the road caught anything.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Croydon Road, Elmers End, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Road Sign Collapse and vehicle debris in road, 5th April
item.description:
On 5th April at around 03:20am, it was raised that a vehicle appeared to have collided with a road sign on Black Prince Roundabout, junction with Bourne Road. A street sign was lying across the road with a large amount of glass and plastic debris scattered around the lanes of the roundabout. The road sign was moved on to the pavement but remains collapsed but there is still a lot of debris in the road.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Bourne Road, Bexley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly tipping again, 5th April
item.description:
These fly tipped items have been here a few weeks now.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Sun Lane, Blackheath
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Deceased cat, 5th April
item.description:
A pet cat is dead in the road
